_title: IEC 60364-7 — Special Locations: Zones, IP, RCD and Bonding Requirements
_note: Part 7 contains requirements additional to or derogating from the general requirements of Parts 1–6. Where Part 7 is silent, the general requirements of Parts 4 and 5 apply. Always read the relevant Part 7 section in full — this file captures the critical design parameters only.

section_701_bath_shower._title: IEC 60364-7-701:2019 — Rooms containing a bath or shower

zone_0.description: Interior of the bath tub or shower tray, or below the floor-level shower area
zone_0.volume: The space enclosed by the walls/floor of the bath or shower — the wet zone itself
permitted_equipment: Fixed SELV luminaires rated for Zone 0
Whirlpool pump units if specifically certified for Zone 0
prohibited: Junction boxes (unless IP67 and only SELV circuits)
zone_0.circuit_types: SELV only, voltage ≤ 12V AC or ≤ 30V DC

zone_1.description: Directly above the bath tub or shower tray, from the finished floor level to 2.25m height; the interior of shower cubicle walls within 1.2m of shower head
zone_1.IP_minimum: IPX4 (splash-proof); IPX5 where jets used for cleaning purposes
permitted_equipment: Fixed luminaires rated IPX4 or IPX5 as appropriate
SELV shaver supply units (IEC 61558-2-5) where located at high level
Whirlpool and spa jet pumps specifically designed for Zone 1
Instantaneous water heaters where specifically designed for Zone 1
zone_1.circuit_types: SELV preferred; PELV and 230V fixed equipment permitted if specifically designed and rated for Zone 1

zone_2.description: 600mm horizontal distance beyond the perimeter of Zone 1 (beyond the bath rim or shower tray edge), and from floor to 2.25m height; washbasin splash areas within 600mm of tap
permitted_equipment: Heated towel rails with suitable IP rating
Exhaust ventilation fan rated IPX4 minimum
zone_2.circuit_types: 230V fixed equipment permitted with appropriate RCD protection

outside_zones.description: Areas outside Zones 0, 1, 2 within the same room
outside_zones.note: Many countries apply Zone 2 requirements to the whole bathroom — verify national requirements

rcd_requirements.scope: All circuits in Zones 0, 1, and 2 must be protected by 30mA RCD. Recommended for all circuits within the bathroom.

supplementary_bonding.scope: All exposed conductive parts (metalwork of shower trays, bath frames, heated towel rails) AND extraneous conductive parts (metallic water pipes, gas pipes, heating pipes, metallic structural parts accessible) within Zones 0, 1, and 2
supplementary_bonding.note: Supplementary bonding connects metal parts together but does not require a connection back to the main earthing terminal — it equalises potential locally. The exposed conductive parts must also be connected to the CPC in the normal way.

zone_0.description: Interior of the pool basin, fountain basin, wading pool
zone_0.circuit_types: FELV at max 12V AC / 30V DC, or SELV

zone_1.description: 2m horizontally from pool edge; up to 2.5m above floor level; below floor level where pool structure accessible
zone_1.IP_minimum: IPX5 (jets used); IPX7 where flooding is possible
zone_1.circuit_types: SELV or Class II fixed equipment only

zone_2.description: Further 1.5m horizontally beyond Zone 1 boundary (3.5m total from pool edge)

fountain_additional.accessible_fountains: Zone 0 where water accessible to persons. SELV ≤12V AC, IPX8.
fountain_additional.inaccessible_decorative_fountains: SELV or PELV ≤25V AC, IPX5 minimum at equipment

section_702_swimming_pools.bonding: All metallic components of pool structure, ladders, handrails, lighting fittings, water treatment equipment, and structural metalwork within 3.5m of pool edge must be bonded

section_704_construction_sites._title: IEC 60364-7-704:2005 — Construction and demolition site installations

rcd_all_socket_outlets.note: Every socket outlet circuit on a construction site must have 30mA RCD protection. No exemptions.

socket_outlet_IP.type: IEC 60309 (industrial pattern) for 16A and above — interlocked socket outlets preferred

disconnection_time_TT.note: Stricter than general TT system (0.2s vs 0.2s already standard for final circuits ≤32A, but important in context of temporary supplies)

supply_systems.TN: Permitted. Ensure Ze is measured and Zs calculations are done for temporary distribution.
supply_systems.TT: Common where supply is from temporary generator or utility pole connection. 30mA RCD mandatory.
supply_systems.IT: Not typically used on construction sites
supply_systems.separation_transformer: 230V output from 110V centre-tapped transformer permitted — reduces shock risk from 230V to 55V. Widely used in UK practice (BS EN 61558-2-23).

portable_tools_voltage.note: 110V CTE (centre-tapped-to-earth) transformers reduce maximum shock voltage from 230V to 55V. Standard practice in UK and many African countries for handheld tools.

key_requirements.distribution_boards_IP: IP44 minimum for all temporary distribution boards and assembly boards
key_requirements.cable_protection: Cables on ground subject to mechanical damage must be armoured or routed in conduit. Overhead cables must be at safe height or protected from vehicle strike.

rcd_fixed_equipment_outdoors.note: Fixed equipment outdoors (irrigation, yard lighting): 300mA RCD for fire protection, or 30mA where persons may be present

IP_minimum.areas_with_livestock: IP44 minimum, IP54 where regular hosing
IP_minimum.areas_with_corrosive_atmosphere: IP54 with corrosion-resistant enclosures (ammonia, hydrogen sulphide from slurry)

metallic_structure_bonding.scope: All metallic structural elements (steel-frame buildings, water troughs, feed bins, milking equipment, metallic fence posts) must be bonded to a common equipotential bonding network
metallic_structure_bonding.note: Step and touch potentials are a significant hazard in agricultural locations — especially in wet conditions with livestock present. Animals are more sensitive to small potential differences than humans.

wiring_systems.exposed_locations: Armoured cable (SWA) or cables in mechanical protection throughout
wiring_systems.rodent_risk: PVC cables in conduit or armoured — rodents cause significant cable damage in agricultural buildings

rcd_per_berth.note: Each berth (each socket outlet position or pedestal) must have its own 30mA RCD. Do not group multiple berths on one RCD — a fault on one boat must not disconnect others.

pedestal_socket_outlets.type: IEC 60309 16A or 32A (blue — 230V, or red — 400V) interlocked socket outlets
pedestal_socket_outlets.note: Interlocked socket outlets prevent live disconnection under load

supply_system.recommended: TT with separate earth electrode at marina
supply_system.TN_note: TN-C-S (PME) is problematic in marinas — broken PEN conductor can cause corrosion of boat hulls. TT is safer.
supply_system.separation_transformer: Isolation transformer per berth eliminates galvanic corrosion risk — best practice for premium marinas

corrosion_protection.earthing_electrodes: Stainless steel or copper-bonded electrodes only — galvanised steel corrodes rapidly in marine environment
corrosion_protection.enclosures: 316 stainless steel or marine-grade GRP for all external enclosures
corrosion_protection.cable_glands: Stainless steel or marine-grade brass only

electric_shock_in_water.note: Electric shock drowning (ESD) can occur from leakage current in marina water from faulty boat shore-power connections. All circuits must have 30mA RCD and installations must be inspected regularly. Earth fault monitoring at distribution board level is recommended.

section_710_medical_locations._status: Under revision — verify with current national adoption

group_0.description: No electrical medical devices applied to patient body

group_1.description: Electrical medical devices applied externally to patient body (non-critical)

requirements.rcd: 30mA RCD on all socket outlet circuits

group_2.description: Life-support and critical care — electrical medical devices invasively connected to patient heart region

requirements.supply_system: IT (isolated) system mandatory using medical IT transformer (IEC 61558-2-15) and Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD/IMS)
requirements.first_fault_behaviour: First earth fault — ALARM only, no disconnection. Supply continuity maintained.
requirements.second_fault_behaviour: Second earth fault — overcurrent protection operates (two faults effectively create a fault current loop)
requirements.IT_system_max_capacity_kVA: Typically 3.15–8kVA per operating room IT transformer
requirements.rcd: 30mA RCD NOT used in Group 2 IT system circuits (would negate the IT protection purpose). Use on non-IT circuits in the same room.
requirements.supplementary_bonding: All exposed and extraneous conductive parts within patient area (within 2.5m of patient) must be bonded to a local equipotential bonding busbar (LPEB)

key_design_points: IT system in Group 2 requires one transformer per 25-50m² maximum (one per operating theatre typically)
IMD alarm panel must be visible to clinical staff at the point of care
Emergency switching off (ESO) button must be accessible and clearly labelled in each Group 2 room
Normal, essential, and critical emergency supplies must be clearly separated and labelled in all distribution boards
Reference: IEC 60364-7-710 and IEC 60601-1 for medical electrical equipment requirements

section_712_solar_pv._title: IEC 60364-7-712:2017 — Solar photovoltaic (PV) power supply systems

dc_wiring.string_protection: String fuses or string combiner diodes where more than one string is connected in parallel — to prevent reverse current in a faulted string
dc_wiring.cable_type: DC PV cable (IEC 62930 / H1Z2Z2-K or equivalent) — UV-resistant, temperature-rated to 120°C
dc_wiring.cable_routing: DC positive and negative cables run together throughout — minimise loop area to reduce EMC and fire risk
dc_wiring.labelling: DC PV cables labelled at all junction boxes and penetrations: 'PV DC — MAY BE LIVE IN DAYLIGHT'

earthing_PV_system.floating_unearthed: Typical for residential and small commercial systems — array floating with AC side grounded. Facilitates insulation monitoring.
earthing_PV_system.functional_earthing_one_pole: Some systems earth one pole (positive or negative) for lightning protection or EMC. Verify inverter compatibility.
earthing_PV_system.structural_mounting: Metal mounting frames must be bonded to main earthing terminal. Lightning protection required for standalone structures per IEC 62305.

dc_disconnection.array_level: DC isolator at array or string combiner level — accessible, lockable
dc_disconnection.inverter_level: DC disconnect at inverter — mandatory for maintenance
dc_disconnection.AC_level: AC isolator between inverter and grid connection point
dc_disconnection.fire_service_disconnect: Where required by local fire authority — typically a rapid shutdown device (RSD) accessible to fire fighters at ground level

inverter_protection.anti_islanding: Grid-connected inverters must disconnect from grid on loss of grid (anti-islanding protection) per IEC 62116
inverter_protection.reconnection_delay: Inverter must remain disconnected for minimum 5 minutes after grid restoration before reconnecting

key_requirements.generation_meter: Bi-directional meter or generation meter required for export tariff measurement and grid connection requirements
key_requirements.rcd_for_pv_circuits: AC output circuit from inverter: 30mA Type A RCD minimum on circuits feeding socket outlets or accessible areas. Type B where significant DC leakage possible from inverter.

mode_1.description: Slow AC charging via standard domestic socket outlet — no dedicated communication or vehicle detection. Plug directly into standard 13A or 16A socket.
mode_1.safety_concern: High risk — not recommended in IEC 60364-7-722 for sustained EV charging. Standard sockets not rated for continuous 12–16A 8-hour loads. Many countries prohibit Mode 1 for EV charging.

mode_2.description: Slow AC charging via standard socket outlet WITH in-cable control box (ICCB) providing pilot signal and protective functions. The ICCB contains a 30mA RCD and pilot signal control.
mode_2.RCD_type: Type A minimum, Type B preferred where ICCB may produce DC leakage
mode_2.circuit_requirement: Dedicated socket outlet circuit sized for continuous 32A. Circuit protective conductor to all EV equipment.

mode_3.description: Dedicated AC EV charging point (EVSE — Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment) connected to fixed installation. Communication between vehicle and EVSE via pilot signal (IEC 61851-1). Standard for home wall boxes and public charging.
mode_3.RCD_type: Type A 30mA minimum. Type B required where DC fault current > 6mA is possible (most modern EVSEs with 3-phase charging).
mode_3.circuit_sizing: Cable sized at 100% of EVSE rated current (Ib = EVSE rating). No diversity unless load management system installed.
mode_3.earthing: CPC mandatory throughout. PME (TN-C-S) supply: verify earth continuity monitoring or install alternative earth electrode to manage PME risk.
mode_3.cable_type: Fixed wiring: copper multi-core cable clipped direct or in trunking. SWA for exposed outdoor routes.

mode_4.description: DC fast charging via dedicated off-board charger. High power DC direct to vehicle battery. Requires specialist AC/DC converter installation.
mode_4.supply: Three-phase AC supply to charger unit — often dedicated transformer
mode_4.RCD: Included within charger unit — verify Type B functionality for DC fault current
mode_4.note: Mode 4 installation is specialist work — outside scope of standard electrical contractor. Manufacturer commissioning required.

cable_sizing_rule.rule: The cable supplying an EVSE must be sized for 100% of the EVSE rated continuous current. No diversity factor is applied to individual EV circuit cables.
cable_sizing_rule.load_management_exception: Where a load management system is installed and its output current is documented, the cable may be sized for the managed maximum current.

worked_example.note: XLPE allows one size smaller. Verify voltage drop especially for long runs to remote parking.

protective_measures.rcd_type_for_DC_leakage: Type B where DC leakage possible (three-phase on-board chargers)
protective_measures.socket_outlet_mode_2_3: Dedicated socket outlet or EVSE only — not shared with other loads
protective_measures.earthing_TN_S: Standard TN-S earthing applies without restriction

earthing_TN_C_S_PME.risk: Broken PEN conductor can raise earth potential — hazardous to vehicle occupant touching vehicle and standing on ground. IEC 60364-7-722 requires measures to mitigate.
options: Install pilot earth conductor monitoring device
Use Type B RCD with earth fault loop impedance monitoring

protective_measures.outdoor_socket_IP: IP44 minimum; IP55 recommended for outdoor public installations
